{% include "header.html" %} {% include "partials/nav.html" %} {% include "partials/search.html" %} {% if page.section == "" %}

HTTP intercepting proxy · TUI

Intercept the web from your terminal.

A fast, keyboard-driven HTTP/HTTPS proxy and security toolkit. Capture, replay, fuzz, scan, or drive it all from an AI agent.

Get started View on GitHub

Every request your client makes lands in History as a flow, one keystroke from Replay, the Fuzzer, or a finding.

Start

Capture your first request

Install, trust the CA, and watch flows land.

 Guide

Proxy, Replay & Fuzzer

Intercept, scope, replay, fuzz, and scan.

 Reference

CLI & query language

Every subcommand, config key, and filter.

One tool, the whole assessment.

History, Intercept, Replay, Fuzzer, the Prism scanner, Param Miner, and Findings live in a single project, every flow one keystroke away.

Keyboard-native A palette-driven TUI built with Crystal. No mouse required, no browser tab to babysit.
Fast A compiled proxy with SQLite-backed projects that stay responsive under load.
Scriptable and AI Everything reachable headless via gori run, and open to AI agents through the built-in MCP server.

From capture to finding.

Capture

Run the proxy, trust the CA once, and every request flows into History with full HTTP/2, WebSocket, and gRPC detail.

Test

Send interesting flows to Replay or the Fuzzer, mine hidden parameters, and let Prism flag issues as you browse.

Report

Triage results into Findings, take notes, and export, or hand the whole project to an agent over MCP.

Or hand it to an agent.

gori has no chat window baked in. It exposes your project over MCP, so any agent — Claude, Codex, Grok — reads your captured traffic and drives the tools through one clean seam. You pick the model; your traffic stays where you put it.

Read the MCP guide →

고리

/go·ri/ · noun, Korean

In English it needs three words. Ring, link, and loop all name the same thing, a piece that holds two separate things together.

Traffic you captured, a request you replayed, a bug you filed: gori keeps them on one chain.
